steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A linear function can be written as $f(x)=mx+b$, where $m$ is the slope and $b=f(0)$.

steps.title: Substitute the values
steps.content: The slope is $\frac{2}{5}$, so $m=\frac{2}{5}$. The line passes through $(0,9)$, so $f(0)=9$ and $b=9$. Therefore $$f(x)=\frac{2}{5}x+9.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: $f(x)=\frac{2}{5}x+9$ is the correct answer. The other three choices use the wrong $y$-intercept, so they fail the point $(0,9)$. A is correct.
